architecture.summary: Godot’s main layer bootstraps the engine and computes frame timing; platform modules supply OS, display, input, storage, and deployment integration. The Android implementation combines C++/JNI adapters with Java/Kotlin hosts, plugin and storage services, and a Gradle-driven exporter/build; iOS and Linux/BSD modules provide parallel platform back ends.

domain.name: Vulkan instance setup
domain.description: The binding models instance setup as an `InstanceCreateInfo` record containing optional application metadata plus enabled layer and extension name arrays.
domain.overview: This is the outer configuration record for the Vulkan API surface represented by the supplied headers.

language.name: C++ mapping of C API types
language.description: `CppType<Vk...>` specializations associate C Vulkan type names with C++ wrapper types.
language.overview: The generated mapping provides a type-level bridge between the C Vulkan ABI names and the C++ API layer.

mechanism.title: Wrapper-type association
mechanism.content: A `CppType` specialization is emitted for each mapped Vulkan C record type.

mechanism.title: Structure-type association
mechanism.content: Many record mappings are accompanied by a `CppType<StructureType, ...>` specialization keyed by the Vulkan structure-type enumerator.

domain.name: Vulkan video session configuration
domain.description: The generated records model a video session through a video profile and standard-header-version information, with separate records for decode and encode operations.
domain.overview: This partition declares video API data contracts; it does not show a video-processing implementation.

language.name: C++ static ABI contracts
language.description: The binding uses a macro-selected assertion facility to verify wrapper ABI layout and copy or move properties at compile time.
language.overview: The static-assertion header checks wrapper size compatibility with C Vulkan handles and selected type traits.

mechanism.title: Wrapper-size contract
mechanism.content: The `Instance` wrapper must have the same size as `VkInstance`.

mechanism.title: Copy and move contracts
mechanism.content: The header checks that `Instance` is copy constructible and nothrow move constructible.

mechanism.title: Macro-selected assertion facility
mechanism.content: `VULKAN_HPP_STATIC_ASSERT` defaults to `static_assert` unless callers configure it differently.

code_snippet.code: VULKAN_HPP_STATIC_ASSERT( sizeof( VULKAN_HPP_NAMESPACE::Instance ) == sizeof( VkInstance ), "handle and wrapper have different size!" );

evidence.symbol: VK_VERSION_1_0 Instance size, copy-construction, and move-construction assertions

evidence.title: Working Draft, Standard for Programming Language C++

language.name: C-compatible guarded headers
language.description: The VI declaration file uses preprocessor guards and conditionally enters `extern "C"` when compiled as C++, so one declaration set can be included by C and C++ translation units.
language.overview: This is the boundary mechanism for the platform extension's C-facing declarations.

mechanism.title: Outer inclusion guard
mechanism.content: `VULKAN_VI_H_` prevents the header body from being expanded more than once in one translation unit.

mechanism.title: Conditional C++ linkage
mechanism.content: The `__cplusplus` branch surrounds declarations with `extern "C"` only for C++ compilation.

mechanism.title: Prototype availability switch
mechanism.content: `VK_NO_PROTOTYPES` and `VK_ONLY_EXPORTED_PROTOTYPES` control whether the direct function prototype is declared.

evidence.title: N1570 Committee Draft — Programming Languages — C

language.name: C ABI structures and opaque state
language.description: C declarations use tagged structures, pointer members, and opaque implementation pointers to express caller-owned descriptors and library-managed state across API boundaries.
language.overview: Vulkan descriptors expose typed fields, while zlib hides its internal state behind a pointer.

mechanism.title: Caller-owned Vulkan descriptor
mechanism.content: VkWaylandSurfaceCreateInfoKHR carries structure type, extension pointer, flags, display, and surface fields into a creation call.

mechanism.title: Opaque compression state
mechanism.content: zlib.h declares struct internal_state and stores its pointer in the public stream state rather than exposing the implementation layout.

mechanism.title: Forward-declared implementation contexts
mechanism.content: Wslay and Zstandard headers declare context structures used through API-level pointers.

evidence.title: ISO/IEC 9899:201x Committee Draft N1570 — Programming languages — C

language.name: C function-pointer API declarations
language.description: C function-pointer declarations expose addressable Vulkan entry points and callback-based frame/event integration without requiring a concrete implementation type.
language.overview: The generated Vulkan headers provide both PFN typedefs and optionally visible direct prototypes.

mechanism.title: Dispatchable creation entry point
mechanism.content: PFN_vkCreateWaylandSurfaceKHR specifies the complete call signature for the extension function.

mechanism.title: Conditional direct declaration
mechanism.content: The same header declares vkCreateWaylandSurfaceKHR only when VK_NO_PROTOTYPES and VK_ONLY_EXPORTED_PROTOTYPES do not suppress it.

code_snippet.code: typedef VkResult (VKAPI_PTR *PFN_vkCreateWaylandSurfaceKHR)(VkInstance instance, const VkWaylandSurfaceCreateInfoKHR* pCreateInfo, const VkAllocationCallbacks* pAllocator, VkSurfaceKHR* pSurface);

language.name: C++ module interface partition
language.description: `vulkan_video.cppm` declares the exported `vulkan_hpp:video` module partition and an exported video namespace.
language.overview: The file is a C++ module interface artifact for the Vulkan video API namespace.

mechanism.title: Partition declaration
mechanism.content: `export module vulkan_hpp:video;` names an exported partition of the `vulkan_hpp` module.

mechanism.title: Namespace export
mechanism.content: The file exports the namespace formed by the Vulkan-Hpp namespace macros.

language.name: C++ single-header implementation selection
language.description: VMA uses preprocessor configuration to place its header implementation in one C++ translation unit while selecting a non-MSVC debug macro before inclusion.
language.overview: The .cpp file is intentionally minimal because VMA bodies are emitted from the included header.

mechanism.title: Implementation emission
mechanism.content: VMA_IMPLEMENTATION is defined before vk_mem_alloc.h is included.

mechanism.title: Build-mode bridge
mechanism.content: When DEBUG_ENABLED is set outside MSVC, the translation unit defines _DEBUG.

mechanism.title: Godot Vulkan dependency
mechanism.content: The patched VMA header includes drivers/vulkan/godot_vulkan.h instead of directly including the upstream Vulkan header.

language.name: C++ standard-library module import
language.description: The Vulkan-Hpp façade contains an import std declaration and other headers test VULKAN_HPP_CXX_MODULE before falling back to textual includes.
language.overview: This is a conditional module-aware inclusion path in the generated C++ headers.

mechanism.title: vulkan.hpp import std
mechanism.content: The façade's inventory includes a standard-library module import declaration.

mechanism.title: VULKAN_HPP_CXX_MODULE branch
mechanism.content: The extension-inspection header avoids its normal includes when the Vulkan-Hpp C++ module macro is defined.

domain.name: Explicit DRM synchronization objects
domain.description: Wayland protocol objects import DRM synchronization timelines and attach acquire and release timeline points to a surface commit.
domain.overview: The schema models synchronization as externally exchanged timeline objects and 64-bit point values.

domain.name: WebSocket framing and event contexts
domain.description: Wslay separates frame-level I/O from event-level message handling through frame contexts, event contexts, callback tables, message sources, and send/control queues.
domain.overview: The implementation is organized around incremental frame reads and writes plus event callbacks.

domain.name: zlib stream compression
domain.description: The bundled zlib sources implement Adler-32 and CRC-32 checksums, DEFLATE compression trees, and inflate state for a public streaming compression interface.
domain.overview: Checksum, compression, and decompression code are separate source modules behind zlib.h.

evidence.title: RFC 1950: ZLIB Compressed Data Format Specification

domain.name: Zstandard stream compression
domain.description: The bundled code provides Zstandard stream compression contexts, decompression contexts, dictionaries, entropy tables, hash-based match finders, and optional worker pools.
domain.overview: The implementation is divided into common, compress, and decompress subtrees.

evidence.title: RFC 8878: Zstandard Compression and the application/zstd Media Type
